settings_item_auth_cookie_secure_desc: Controls whether auth and share-verification cookies are only sent over HTTPS. You can disable it for temporary plain-HTTP internal testing, but it should stay enabled in production.
settings_item_auth_allow_user_registrati: Controls whether new users can create accounts from the public sign-up flow. When disabled, only the initial setup flow and admin-created accounts remain available.
settings_item_auth_register_activation_e: Controls whether new users must confirm their email address before they can sign in for the first time.
settings_item_auth_access_token_ttl_secs: Controls how long newly issued access tokens stay valid after login or refresh.
settings_item_auth_refresh_token_ttl_sec: Controls how long refresh tokens stay valid before users must sign in again.
settings_item_auth_register_activation_t: Controls how long registration activation links remain usable before they expire.
settings_item_auth_contact_change_ttl_se: Controls how long email change confirmation links remain usable before they expire.
settings_item_auth_password_reset_ttl_se: Controls how long password reset links remain usable before they expire.
settings_item_auth_contact_verification_: The minimum wait between verification email resend attempts for the same user.
settings_item_auth_password_reset_reques: The minimum wait between password reset email requests for the same user.
settings_item_default_storage_quota_desc: The default storage quota assigned to newly created users. Use 0 for unlimited.
settings_item_gravatar_base_url_desc: The base URL used to build Gravatar avatar requests. Replace it with a reachable proxy or mirror if the default service is inaccessible.
